advent.name: Advent
advent.description: The four-week season of preparation and expectation before Christmas. The Church looks back to Christ's first coming and forward to his promised return, cultivating watchfulness, repentance, and hope.

readings.label: Isaiah 9:2-7
readings.note: A child is born — the Prince of Peace

readings.label: Isaiah 40:1-11
readings.note: Comfort my people — prepare the way of the Lord

readings.label: Isaiah 7:14
readings.note: The virgin shall conceive — Immanuel

readings.label: Malachi 3:1-4
readings.note: The messenger who prepares the way

readings.label: Luke 1:26-38
readings.note: The Annunciation to Mary

readings.label: Luke 1:46-55
readings.note: The Magnificat — Mary's song of praise

readings.label: Matthew 3:1-12
readings.note: John the Baptist prepares the way

readings.label: Romans 13:11-14
readings.note: The day is at hand — put on the armor of light

christmas.name: Christmas
christmas.description: The celebration of Christ's birth — God taking on human flesh. The season extends twelve days from December 25 through January 5, celebrating the Incarnation and its meaning for all creation.

readings.label: Luke 2:1-20
readings.note: The birth of Jesus and the shepherds

readings.label: Matthew 1:18-25
readings.note: Joseph and the birth of Immanuel

readings.label: John 1:1-14
readings.note: The Word became flesh

readings.label: Isaiah 52:7-10
readings.note: How beautiful the feet of those who bring good news

readings.label: Titus 2:11-14
readings.note: The grace of God has appeared

readings.label: Galatians 4:4-7
readings.note: God sent his Son, born of a woman

epiphany.name: Epiphany
epiphany.description: The season of revelation and manifestation. Beginning January 6 with the visit of the Magi, Epiphany celebrates Christ being revealed to the nations — through his baptism, his first miracle, and his public ministry.

readings.label: Matthew 2:1-12
readings.note: The visit of the Magi

readings.label: Matthew 3:13-17
readings.note: The baptism of Jesus

readings.label: John 2:1-11
readings.note: The wedding at Cana — first sign

readings.label: Isaiah 60:1-6
readings.note: Arise, shine, for your light has come

readings.label: Ephesians 3:1-12
readings.note: The mystery of Christ revealed to the Gentiles

readings.label: Matthew 17:1-9
readings.note: The Transfiguration

lent.name: Lent
lent.description: A forty-day season of repentance, fasting, and preparation for Easter, beginning on Ash Wednesday. The Church follows Jesus into the wilderness, examining sin, practicing self-denial, and deepening dependence on God's grace.

readings.label: Matthew 4:1-11
readings.note: The temptation of Jesus in the wilderness

readings.label: Psalm 51:1-17
readings.note: Create in me a clean heart, O God

readings.label: Joel 2:12-17
readings.note: Return to the Lord with all your heart

readings.label: Isaiah 53:1-12
readings.note: The Suffering Servant

readings.label: 2 Corinthians 5:20-6:10
readings.note: Be reconciled to God

readings.label: John 3:14-21
readings.note: God so loved the world

readings.label: Psalm 130:1-8
readings.note: Out of the depths I cry to you, O Lord

readings.label: Hebrews 4:14-16
readings.note: A great high priest — approach the throne of grace

holy_week.name: Holy Week
holy_week.description: The most solemn week of the Christian year, from Palm Sunday through Holy Saturday. The Church walks with Jesus through his triumphal entry, the Last Supper, his agony in Gethsemane, trial, crucifixion, and burial.

readings.label: Matthew 21:1-11
readings.note: The triumphal entry — Palm Sunday

readings.label: John 13:1-17
readings.note: Jesus washes the disciples' feet

readings.label: Matthew 26:17-30
readings.note: The Last Supper

readings.label: Matthew 26:36-46
readings.note: Gethsemane — not my will, but yours

readings.label: Matthew 27:27-54
readings.note: The crucifixion

readings.label: John 19:16-37
readings.note: It is finished

readings.label: Psalm 22:1-21
readings.note: My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?

readings.label: Isaiah 52:13-53:12
readings.note: He was pierced for our transgressions

easter.name: Easter
easter.description: The great fifty-day celebration of Christ's resurrection, the central event of Christian faith. From Easter Sunday through the day before Ascension, the Church rejoices that death has been conquered and new life has begun.

readings.label: Matthew 28:1-10
readings.note: The resurrection — he is not here, he is risen

readings.label: John 20:1-18
readings.note: Mary Magdalene at the empty tomb

readings.label: Luke 24:13-35
readings.note: The road to Emmaus

readings.label: 1 Corinthians 15:1-11
readings.note: The resurrection appearances

readings.label: 1 Corinthians 15:50-58
readings.note: Death is swallowed up in victory

readings.label: Romans 6:3-11
readings.note: United with Christ in his resurrection

readings.label: Colossians 3:1-4
readings.note: Set your minds on things above

ascension.name: Ascension
ascension.description: The ten-day period from Ascension Day (39 days after Easter) to Pentecost Eve. Christ ascends to the right hand of the Father, is given all authority, and promises to send the Holy Spirit. The Church waits in prayer and expectation.

readings.label: Acts 1:1-11
readings.note: The ascension — he will come back in the same way

readings.label: Ephesians 1:15-23
readings.note: Seated at the right hand, far above all rule

readings.label: Psalm 110:1-4
readings.note: Sit at my right hand

readings.label: Hebrews 1:1-4
readings.note: The Son is the radiance of God's glory

readings.label: Daniel 7:13-14
readings.note: The Son of Man given dominion and glory

readings.label: Philippians 2:5-11
readings.note: God exalted him to the highest place

pentecost.name: Pentecost
pentecost.description: The celebration of the Holy Spirit's coming upon the Church, fifty days after Easter. Often called the birthday of the Church, Pentecost marks the fulfillment of Christ's promise to send the Spirit as teacher, comforter, and empowerer.

readings.label: Acts 2:1-21
readings.note: The coming of the Holy Spirit

readings.label: Joel 2:28-32
readings.note: I will pour out my Spirit on all flesh

readings.label: John 14:15-27
readings.note: The promise of the Holy Spirit

readings.label: John 16:5-15
readings.note: The Spirit will guide you into all truth

readings.label: Romans 8:1-17
readings.note: Life in the Spirit

readings.label: 1 Corinthians 12:4-13
readings.note: Varieties of gifts, one Spirit

ordinary.name: Ordinary Time
ordinary.description: The longest season of the church year, filling the weeks between Pentecost and Advent. 'Ordinary' comes from 'ordinal' (counted), not 'commonplace.' This season focuses on Christian discipleship, growth, and the teachings of Jesus for daily life.

readings.label: Psalm 1:1-6
readings.note: Blessed is the one who delights in the law of the Lord

readings.label: Psalm 119:105-112
readings.note: Your word is a lamp to my feet

readings.label: Matthew 28:16-20
readings.note: The Great Commission

readings.label: Matthew 5:1-12
readings.note: The Beatitudes

readings.label: Micah 6:6-8
readings.note: What does the Lord require of you?

readings.label: James 1:22-27
readings.note: Be doers of the word, not hearers only
